paper_title: Prevalence of Ragweed Allergy in Hungarian Schoolchildren

methods.description: Process for collecting data on ragweed allergy and related factors using standardized questionnaires.

steps.description: Questionnaires were sent to every school in the country where at least 10 children attended the 3rd class.
The questionnaires were completed by the parents anonymously and only data related to ragweed pollen allergy were analysed.

methods.description: Monitoring and analysis of airborne ragweed pollen concentration using a network of monitoring stations.

steps.description: The airborne pollen concentration of ragweed was monitored by 19 monitoring stations of the National Aerobiological Network.
Air samples were continuously collected by Hirst-type pollen traps and processed for analysis.
The average sum of ragweed pollen concentration was calculated from the summarized daily pollen level per each year.

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data and assess associations between health outcomes and risk factors.

steps.description: Children who had changed their place of residence since birth were excluded from the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: filter data for children with consistent residence

steps.description: Descriptive statistical methods were used to demonstrate prevalence data, and Chi-square tests were applied to compare frequencies.
steps.searchable_summary: perform descriptive statistics and Chi-square tests

steps.description: Associations between health outcomes and potential risk factors were assessed using binary logistic regression analyses.
